sections.paragraph:
fut. יִיבַשׁ, pl. יִבָשׁוּ inf. constr. יְבֹושׁ, יְבשֶׁת Gen 8:7, to be dried up, to be or become dry; used of plants, trees, grass, Isa 15:6 19:7 40:7 , Isa 40:8 Joe 1:12 of tilled fields, Jer 23:10 Isa 27:11 Eze 17:9 of the earth, Gen 8:14 of bones void of marrow, Eze 37:11 of a withered hand, 1Ki 13:4 (comp. Mar 3:1); hence, of vital strength, Psa 22:16, יָבֵשׁ כַּחֶרֶשׂ כֹּחִי “my strengthis dried up like a potsherd.” Moisture itself is said also to dry up; hence it is used of rivers and the sea, Job 14:11 Gen 8:14 Joe 1:20. (Arab. يَبِسَ id.) As to its difference from הָרֵב to be dry, see under that word.

Piel יִבֵּשׁ to dry, to make dry, Job 15:30 Pro 17:22 Nah 1:4 (where וַיַבְּשֵׁהוּ is for וַיְיַבְּשֵׁהוּ).

Hiphil הֹובִישּׁ-

(1) to dry, to make dry, Jos 2:10 4:23 .

(2) intransit. to become dry, used of plants, fruits, the harvest, Joe 1:10, 12 Joe 1:12, 17 Joe 1:17and metaph. ver. 12 Joe 1:12, “joy is dried up (i.e. has perished) from the children of men.”

II. יָבַשׁ Hiphil הֹובִישׁ, with a signification taken from that of בֹּושׁ-

(1) to put to shame, 2Sa 19:6.

(2) intrans, i.q. בֹּושׁ in Kal, to be ashamed, to be put to shame, made to blush, Jer 2:26 6:15 8:12 especially used of a person whose hope has failed, JoeI 1:11 Jer 10:14 Zec 9:5 poet. of cities overthrown (compare the Germ. zu Schanden werden), Jer 48:1, 20 Jer 48:20 50:2 , Jer 50:3.

(3) to do shameful things, to act basely, Hos 2:7.

יבש

adj. fem. יְבֵשָׁה-

(1) dry, dried up, Job 13:25 Eze 17:24 21:3 .

(2) [Jabesh], pr.n.-(a) of a town in Gilead, which also is written יָבֵישׁ 1Sa 11:1 1:3 Jud. 21:8 .-(b) of a man, 2Ki 15:10, 13 2Ki 15:13, 14 2Ki 15:14.
